channel.title: Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare
channel.description: Recent content in Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare

item.title: 04_lec_handout.pdf
item.description: Lecture slides on Organic Structure Determination: Infrared Spectroscopy (IR) and MS Fragmentation Patterns.

item.title: 05_lec_5_2006.pdf
item.description: Lecture slides on Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) Spectroscopy.

item.title: 06_lec_6_7_2006.pdf
item.description: Lecture slides on NMR Spectroscopy ? Spin-Spin Coupling and Connectivity.

item.title: 07_lec_7_2006.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes with reference material for the hour exam on Friday, September 29.

item.title: 08_lec_8_06_ans.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Organic Structure Elucidation. Images of NMR graphs removed due to copyright restrictions. Please see: McMurry, John. Fundamentals of Organic Chemistry. 6th ed. Pacific Grove, CA: Thomson-Brooks/Cole, 2007. ISBN: 0495012033.

item.title: 08_lec_8_2006.pdf
item.description: This resource contains information on Images of NMR graphs removed due to copyright restrictions. Please see: McMurry, John. Fundamentals of Organic Chemistry. 6th ed. Pacific Grove, CA: Thomson-Brooks/Cole, 2007. ISBN: 0495012033.

item.title: 09_lec_9_2006.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Organic Structure Determination.

item.title: 09_lec_9_ans_06.pdf
item.description: Answers to problems from Lecture #9.

item.title: 12_lec_12_2006.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Conjugated Systems, Molecular Orbital Theory, and Native Chemical Ligation.

item.title: 13_lec_13_14_06.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Conjugated Systems, Molecular Orbital Theory, and Electrocyclic Reactions.

item.title: 17_lec_17_18_06.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Pericyclic Reactions - Cycloadditions, Sigmatropic Rearrangements.

item.title: 19_lec_19_20_06.pdf
item.description: Lecture slides on Amines.

item.title: jw_1_2_2.pdf
item.description: Reaction overview document on [2+2] Photocycloadditions in Organic Chemistry, developed by teaching assistant Jeewoo Lim.

item.title: jw_2.pdf
item.description: Reaction overview document on Reactions to Know, developed by teaching assistant Jeewoo Lim.

item.title: l22_5_13_notes.pdf
item.description: This resource contains information on topics michael reaction, mechanism, thermodynamics and robinson annulation.

item.title: lec_15_16_17_06.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Pericyclic Reactions, Electrocyclic Processes, Cycloaddition Reactions, Woodward-Hoffmann Rules for Cycloadditions, and Coefficients of Frontier Molecular Orbitals.

item.title: lec_21_22_23_24.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Carboxylic Acids and Derivatives.

item.title: lec_25_26_27_28.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Enols and Enolates.

item.title: lec_29_30_31_32.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes with an Outline and Study Guide for the unit on Carbocations.

item.title: Lecture Notes
item.description:
LEC # TOPICS
L1 Molecular Formula and Index of Hydrogen Deficiency [EA] (PDF)
L2 Molecular Formula and Index of Hydrogen Deficiency [MS] (cont.)
L3 Functional Groups [IR]
L4 Functional Groups: Fragmentation [MS] (PDF)
L5 Functional Groups [13C NMR] (PDF)
L6 Connectivity [1H NMR] (PDF)
L7 Connectivity (cont.) [1 H NMR] (PDF)
L8

Organic Structure Elucidation (PDF)

Answers (PDF)
L9

Organic Structure Elucidation (cont.) (PDF)

item.title: lectures_33_34.pdf
item.description: Lecture notes on Radicals.

item.title: pka_values.pdf
item.description: Section 23-6 text and Table 23-2 removed due to copyright restrictions. Please see: Kemp, Daniel and Vellaccio, Frank. Organic Chemistry. New York, N.Y.: Worth Publishers, c1980. ISBN: 0879011238.

item.title: unit_1_study.pdf
item.description: Assignment and Study Guide for Unit I: Methods for the Elucidation of the Structure of Organic Compounds.

item.title: unit_2_study.pdf
item.description: Assignment and Study Guide for Unit II: The Chemistry of Ethers, Epoxides, and Sulfides.

item.title: unit_3_study.pdf
item.description: Assignment and Study Guide for Unit III: Molecular Orbital Theory, Pericyclic Reactions, and the Woodward-Hoffmann Rules.
